Maintenance Playbooks Drive Lab Scripts on Hosts
App Deploy Three-Play Pattern (Provision + Deploy + Wire)
Stack Host Provisioning Pipeline: find-or-create-host to generate-ip to ip-to-vmid to lxc-create
Docker Role Install-Configure Pipeline: main to install to config to handler
Guest Bootstrap plus Unattended Upgrades plus Ntfy Notification Integration
UI-Agnostic Job Runner Parity (Semaphore + Rundeck same jobs)

nodes.label: Stack Model (Docker Apps Grouped on Shared Hosts)
nodes.norm_label: stack model (docker apps grouped on shared hosts)

nodes.label: Template Docker Tasks: Deploy Docker App
nodes.norm_label: template docker tasks: deploy docker app

nodes.label: Template Native Handler: Restart APP_NAME via systemd
nodes.norm_label: template native handler: restart app_name via systemd

nodes.label: Template Native Tasks: Deploy Native LXC App
nodes.norm_label: template native tasks: deploy native lxc app

nodes.label: Bootstrap Task: Configure Proxmox Backup Server
nodes.norm_label: bootstrap task: configure proxmox backup server

nodes.label: Task: Derive VMID from IP Address
nodes.norm_label: task: derive vmid from ip address

nodes.label: Task: Find or Create Stack Host
nodes.norm_label: task: find or create stack host

nodes.label: Docker Role as Mandatory Dependency for Docker Apps
nodes.rationale: Template Docker role always declares role: docker as a dependency, ensuring Docker Engine is installed before any Docker app role runs. This enforces correct install ordering without requiring each app role to repeat the install steps.
nodes.norm_label: docker role as mandatory dependency for docker apps

nodes.label: config/.generated/facts.yml Incremental Bootstrap State File
nodes.rationale: Bootstrap writes service connection details incrementally after each baseline service deploys. Subsequent services and app deploys read from this via homelabinfra_infra. Gitignored so secrets never leak. Re-running bootstrap regenerates it.

nodes.label: Watchtower-Ntfy Feedback Loop for Container Updates
nodes.rationale: Watchtower notifies via Ntfy when a container is updated. Users correlate with Uptime Kuma DOWN alerts to detect breakage, then run Rollback Container job. This avoids building custom update logic: Watchtower does it; we configure the notification path.
nodes.norm_label: watchtower-ntfy feedback loop for container updates

nodes.label: VMID Derived from IP Address
nodes.rationale: VMID is computed from IP octets (prefix + third + fourth octet) so it is deterministic and avoids VMID collision management. DHCP hosts must explicitly provide a vmid since no IP is available to derive from.

nodes.label: Stack Host Find-or-Create Pattern
nodes.rationale: Docker apps that belong to the same stack share a host LXC. The first app to deploy provisions the host; subsequent apps find it by Proxmox tag (tag_<stack_name>) and reuse it. This avoids N+1 containers for related apps while keeping deploy playbooks app-centric.

nodes.label: Guest Bootstrap Idempotency via homelab_bootstrapped Fact
nodes.rationale: Bootstrap runs once per new guest, guarded by a local fact file. The calling play checks this fact so re-running a deploy does not re-run full bootstrap. Ensures idempotency without a central state store.
nodes.norm_label: guest bootstrap idempotency via homelab_bootstrapped fact

nodes.label: Wiring Pattern (register app with platform services)
nodes.rationale: Every app deployment ends by registering with platform services (reverse proxy, SSO, DNS, uptime monitor). Each wiring task is conditional on its configured provider — missing providers are no-ops, not errors. This allows the platform to be flexible and composable without requiring all services.
nodes.norm_label: wiring pattern (register app with platform services)

nodes.label: Unwiring Pattern (inverse of wiring, idempotent removal)
nodes.rationale: Every unwiring task is the inverse of its wiring counterpart. All are idempotent no-ops when the resource does not exist, so remove.yml can safely re-run without errors.
nodes.norm_label: unwiring pattern (inverse of wiring, idempotent removal)

nodes.label: Semaphore/Rundeck Job Parity
nodes.rationale: Both Semaphore and Rundeck are supported with identical job sets. Playbooks are UI-agnostic, job definitions are just importable wrappers.

nodes.label: Per-Instance Config File Pattern
nodes.rationale: Instance config filename becomes the hostname, Caddy subdomain, and Authentik app name. Persists after removal as a restore point.
